The No-Bid Contract That Is Turning Washington’s Reflecting Pool Blue
The Lincoln Memorial’s reflecting pool is an icon, the unchanging backdrop to generations of concerts and protests. Now, Trump is changing it, by painting its bottom “American flag blue.”
The paint job also symbolizes something else: how Trump is bypassing decades-old regulations to remake Washington, and to hand out no-bid contracts to his preferred firms.

The contract for the work was given to a firm that Trump said had worked on the pool at his golf club in Northern Virginia. The Trump administration said it had no time to seek out other bidders: any delay would bring “serious injury” to the government.

Trump’s administration also did not submit the change to a commission that reviews changes to Washington’s monuments. As a result, experts say, it’s unclear if the new paint job will enhance the pool’s look — or seem jarringly artificial.
One other worry: algae could quickly obscure Trump’s new paint job. His repairs are not tackling a plumbing problem that hampers the pool’s filtration, leading to ugly green blooms.
Allison Robbert for The New York Times
